name: perl
title: perl
summary: Larry Wall's Practical Extraction and Report Language
description:
Larry Wall's Practical Extraction and Report Language
Perl is a highly capable, feature-rich programming language with over
20 years of development. Perl 5 runs on over 100 platforms from
portables to mainframes. Perl is suitable for both rapid prototyping
and large scale development projects.
.
Perl 5 supports many programming styles, including procedural,
functional, and object-oriented. In addition to this, it is supported
by an ever-growing collection of reusable modules which accelerate
development. Some of these modules include Web frameworks, database
integration, networking protocols, and encryption. Perl provides
interfaces to C and C++ for custom extension development.

commands.name: corelist
commands.description: a commandline frontend to Module::CoreList

arguments.description: lists all versions of the given module (or the matching modules, in case you used a module
arguments.raw: -a  lists all versions of the given module (or the matching modules, in case you used a module

arguments.description: finds the first perl version where a module has been released by date, and not by version
arguments.raw: -d  finds the first perl version where a module has been released by date, and not by version

arguments.description: Given two versions of perl, this prints a human-readable table of all module changes be-
arguments.raw: --diff  Given two versions of perl, this prints a human-readable table of all module changes be-

arguments.description: lists the first version bundle of each named feature given
arguments.raw: -f  lists the first version bundle of each named feature given

arguments.description: lists the first version bundle of each named feature given
arguments.raw: --feature  lists the first version bundle of each named feature given

arguments.description: lists all of the perl releases and when they were released
arguments.raw: -r  lists all of the perl releases and when they were released

arguments.description: Shows if the given module is primarily maintained in perl core or on CPAN and bug tracker
arguments.raw: -u  Shows if the given module is primarily maintained in perl core or on CPAN and bug tracker

arguments.description: Shows if the given module is primarily maintained in perl core or on CPAN and bug tracker
arguments.raw: --upstream  Shows if the given module is primarily maintained in perl core or on CPAN and bug tracker

arguments.description: lists the first version of perl each named utility program was released with
arguments.raw: --utils  lists the first version of perl each named utility program was released with

arguments.description: lists all of the perl release versions we got the CoreList for.
arguments.raw: -v  lists all of the perl release versions we got the CoreList for.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use corelist because Larry Wall's Practical Extraction and Report Language.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use corelist as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `corelist -v` as documented upstream for corelist.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `corelist [-a|-d] <ModuleName> | /<ModuleRegex>/ [<ModuleVersion>] ...` as documented upstream for corelist.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `corelist [-v <PerlVersion>] [ <ModuleName> | /<ModuleRegex>/ ] ...` as documented upstream for corelist.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `corelist [-r <PerlVersion>] ...` as documented upstream for corelist.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair corelist with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `corelist --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: corelist - a commandline frontend to Module::CoreList
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install perl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `corelist -v`. Further invocations from upstream: `corelist [-a|-d] <ModuleName> | /<ModuleRegex>/ [<ModuleVersion>] ...`; `corelist [-v <PerlVersion>] [ <ModuleName> | /<ModuleRegex>/ ] ...`. Larry Wall's Practical Extraction and Report Language.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Larry Wall's Practical Extraction and Report Language.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: corelist - a commandline frontend to Module::CoreList.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install perl
Install with: sudo apt install perl
